title: Selectors, Cardinality, Unknown Values, and Typed Extraction · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs selectors, cardinality, unknown values, and typed extraction from revision, authorization, seed, URL, policy, response bytes, parsed identity, record provenance, frontier transition, Promise settlement, browser state, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for runtime-validated extraction contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting.
Represent absent, empty, malformed, duplicate, conflicting, and admitted values as distinct outcomes.
Keep attributes, text, JSON-LD, configuration, and plugin output unknown until validators produce domain values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Selector Contract · changed case · Review Selector Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 8413 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove selector cardinality, missingness, unknown runtime validation, field normalization, and drift alarms; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use fixtures, cardinality histograms, rejection rates, schema versions, and changed markup to surface selector drift. Artifact under review: runtime-validated extraction contract.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Drift Detection, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Drift Detection
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Drift Detection: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review TypeScript team handles case 8604 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove selector cardinality, missingness, unknown runtime validation, field normalization, and drift alarms; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use fixtures, cardinality histograms, rejection rates, schema versions, and changed markup to surface selector drift.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Drift Detection.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Drift Detection, keep Drift Detection connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use fixtures, cardinality histograms, rejection rates, schema versions, and changed markup to surface selector drift.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Drift Detection, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Drift Detection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Drift Detection, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review TypeScript team handles case 8604 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove selector cardinality, missingness, unknown runtime validation, field normalization, and drift alarms; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use fixtures, cardinality histograms, rejection rates, schema versions, and changed markup to surface selector drift.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Drift Detection, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Selectors either work or throw when site markup changes.
